summary: The dfr output logic was incorrect: it only asserted dfr for one cycle when prev_level < curr_level, but the reference likely expects dfr to remain high as long as the water level is above the previous level (i.e., after a rising transition, dfr stays high until the water level drops again). The fix is to introduce a state variable (dfr_active) that is set when the water level rises and cleared when it falls, so dfr remains asserted as long as the current level is above the previous lowest point. Also, curr_level should be registered (sequential) to avoid combinational/sequential race issues. This patch rewrites the state machine to use registered curr_level and prev_level, and manages dfr_active accordingly.
risks: If the reference expects dfr to be asserted for only one cycle on a rising transition, this patch may cause dfr to remain high longer than intended. However, the problem description and testbench suggest dfr should remain high as long as the water level is above the previous lowest point.
There may be subtle differences in how the reference handles simultaneous sensor changes or glitches, but this patch should match the intended stateful behavior.
next_steps: Run simulation to verify that dfr now matches the reference output.
If mismatches persist, probe the dfr, curr_level, prev_level, and dfr_active signals in the waveform to further refine the state machine.
If all outputs match, proceed to final acceptance and synthesis checks.
